key_insight: In WTaCrV refractory high-entropy alloy, chemical disorder can increase primary radiation defects, yet solid-solution and local chemical ordering help preserve strength and flow stress under irradiation.
engineering_relevance: For nuclear structural materials, controlling short-range chemical order may be as important as composition for improving irradiation tolerance and maintaining mechanical performance.
method: Atomistic modeling compared three structures: Average-Atom (AA), random solid-solution (RSS), and local chemical order (LCO).
Collision cascade simulations quantified Frenkel-pair production at equal radiation dose.
Mechanical response after irradiation was evaluated to link defect generation with strength and flow-stress degradation.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a defect trend of NRSS > NLCO > NAA at the same dose, while RSS and LCO largely suppress irradiation-induced mechanical degradation relative to AA.
watch_item: The conclusions come from atomistic simulations with newly developed potentials, so transferability to real microstructures, doses, and experimental conditions still needs validation.
paper_title: Primary damage and mechanical degradation of WTaCrV refractory high-entropy alloy: effects of solid-solution and chemical ordering
